title: sarcostemma lehmannii
intro: Sarcostemma lehmannii, also known as the climbing staff vine, is a perennial climber that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, it is used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Sarcostemma lehmannii are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and powders, which can be consumed orally or applied topically.
